    Home › Aircraft › Ilyushin Il-62

    Ilyushin Il-62

    Ilyushin · Narrowbody · Nearly retired

    The USSR's intercontinental jet.

    Length53.12 m
    Wingspan43.2 m
    Range10,000 km
    MTOW165 t
    Typical seats186
    Built292
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Seats per metre#22 of 30
    3.50 seats/m typical seats ÷ fuselage length
    Range per tonne#10 of 40
    60.6 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#35 of 43
    0.81 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Ilyushin Il-62's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    Ilyushin Il-6243.2 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m
    #31 of 43 by length → #31 by weight → #24 by speed →
    Specification

    The numbers

    Length53.12 m
    Wingspan43.20 m
    Height12.35 m
    MTOW (Il-62M)165,000 kg
    Range (Il-62M)≈10,000 km
    Typical seatsup to 186
    Engines4 × Soloviev D-30KU, rear-mounted
    First flight3 January 1963
    Built292

    Compare it

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    The Il-62 gave the Soviet Union its first true intercontinental jet — Moscow to Havana, New York and Tokyo — with all four engines clustered on the rear fuselage under a T-tail, the largest aircraft ever built in that configuration. So tail-heavy was the layout that it parked leaning on a deployable fourth leg under the tail.

    It was engineered with remarkable simplicity — manual flight controls operated by aerodynamic tabs, no hydraulics needed — and served as the long-haul flagship for more than a dozen national carriers. 292 were built.

    It has nearly vanished: North Korea's Air Koryo keeps the last passenger examples airworthy, alongside a couple of Russian state machines — flying museum pieces from 1963 still crossing continents.

    Variants

    The Ilyushin Il-62 line

    Il-62
    The original, 1963.
    Il-62M
    The definitive longer-range version — most survivors.
    Operators

    Who flies the Ilyushin Il-62

    The top 2 operators by fleet size, of ≈2 worldwide — the rest are aggregated below. Counts marked ≈ are approximate, verified 2026-07-14.

    KPAir KoryoNorth Korea's flag carrier — the last passenger Il-62s in the world.≈2
    RURussian state operatorsA couple of government and military machines linger.≈2

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
